agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands no location sharing due to driver retention problems. This is a fundamental business conflict that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Last-Mile Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery person transporting a package from origin to customer destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates captured and transmitted at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The period from pickup/dispatch through final delivery during which tracking would be active."},{"term":"Location granularity","definition":"The precision level at which a courier's position is exposed — ranges from exact GPS coordinates to approximate zone or ETA-only."},{"term":"Active delivery","definition":"The state in which a courier has possession of a package and is in transit to the customer."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and customer sees no movement","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — depends on whether location is shown at all, and if so, at what granularity"},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route (multi-stop, personal errand)","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS would expose this; approximate/ETA-only would not — resolution depends on which team's requirement wins"},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer's home but delivering to a neighbor or completing another stop first","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS reveals courier is 'right there' but not arriving, causing customer frustration and support calls"},{"scenario":"Courier works in a sensitive area (domestic violence shelter, government facility)","expected_behavior":"Exact location sharing could create safety and compliance risks for the courier"},{"scenario":"Off-duty or between-delivery location leakage","expected_behavior":"Must never expose courier location outside an active delivery regardless of resolution — this is a privacy invariant"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Both sides carry financial risk. Losing driver retention increases recruitment and training costs (Operations estimates this is already happening). Losing delivery transparency may reduce customer conversion or satisfaction (Customer Success positions this as a differentiator). Without data on churn rates for both sides, the financial calculus is unresolvable here.","operational":"Exact GPS tracking increases infrastructure load (frequent location updates, WebSocket connections). Driver surveillance perception impacts morale, availability, and willingness to accept shifts. Approximate tracking or ETA-based models are operationally lighter and less invasive.","user":"Customers want delivery predictability — they want to know WHEN, not necessarily WHERE. Exact GPS may actually create anxiety (watching a dot not move). Couriers are also users of this platform; their experience and trust directly affects service quality.","risk":"HIGH. Shipping either requirement without resolving the contradiction guarantees organizational damage. If we show exact GPS: driver attrition accelerates, Operations loses trust in product team. If we hide location entirely: Customer Success loses trust, and a promised differentiator is killed. Privacy and labor regulation risk exists if exact GPS is shared without courier consent frameworks.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Desire delivery visibility; exact form (GPS vs. ETA vs. zone) is the open question. Most industry research shows customers primarily want accurate ETAs, not raw coordinates."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected by surveillance perception. Retention is already declining per Operations. Their consent and comfort level is a hard constraint in many jurisdictions."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Has positioned transparency as a market differentiator; needs a solution that demonstrably improves customer experience."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns driver supply and retention; needs a solution that does not worsen driver churn."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance (not yet consulted)","impact":"GPS tracking of workers has regulatory implications (GDPR, state labor laws, worker privacy rights). This stakeholder MUST be involved before any decision."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic priority decision, not a feature decision."}]},"confidence":{"level":30,"reasoning":"Confidence is critically low because the spec contains a direct, unresolved contradiction between two authoritative stakeholder teams. Both requirements are presented as non-negotiable, making them mutually exclusive as stated. No executive tiebreaker has weighed in. Legal/compliance has not been consulted on worker GPS sharing. Without resolution of this contradiction, any acceptance criteria I define would be arbitrary — I would be making a business strategy decision that is above my authority. The spec is not implementable as written.","high_confidence_areas":["Courier location must NEVER be exposed outside an active delivery window — this is a privacy invariant regardless of resolution","The core customer need is delivery predictability, not necessarily raw GPS coordinates","Driver consent and privacy regulations are hard constraints that must be addressed","This conflict requires executive arbitration, not engineering compromise"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder requirement takes priority","Whether a middle-ground solution (approximate location, ETA-only) would satisfy both teams","The actual financial impact of driver churn vs. customer transparency metrics","Regulatory requirements for courier GPS sharing in applicable jurisdictions"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their constraints (drivers ARE leaving; customers DO value transparency)","No executive decision has yet been made to prioritize one team over the other","Legal/compliance review has not been performed on courier location sharing"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers. These requirements are mutually exclusive as stated. This is not a technical tradeoff — it is a business strategy conflict that must be resolved by executive leadership before any specification can be written.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Legal/Compliance","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Side with Customer Success — show exact GPS. Accept driver retention risk.","B: Side with Operations — show no location. Accept competitive differentiation loss.","C: Executive-brokered compromise — show approximate location (neighborhood-level zone or street-level, not exact coordinates) with courier opt-in, providing customer value while reducing surveillance perception. Industry standard (Uber, DoorDash) uses smoothed approximate location, not raw GPS.","D: ETA-only model — show predicted arrival time updated in real-time without any map pin. Fully addresses Operations concern, partially addresses Customer Success need."],"recommendation":"Option C is the industry-proven approach. Recommend executive meeting with both teams plus Legal to define acceptable granularity. The framing should shift from 'show location vs. don't show location' to 'what level of delivery visibility maximizes customer satisfaction WITHOUT creating a surveillance dynamic for couriers.' Most logistics platforms have landed on smoothed approximate location with time-based ETA as the primary signal. However, I CANNOT set acceptance criteria until this is resolved — I am flagging this as a hard stop."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and compliance review of courier GPS data sharing has not been performed. In many jurisdictions (EU under GDPR, California under CCPA, various labor laws), sharing worker location data with third parties requires explicit consent frameworks, data minimization, and purpose limitation. This is not optional.","affected_stakeholders":["Couriers","Legal/Compliance","Executive leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal/Compliance before defining any tracking feature","Proceed without legal review (NOT recommended)"],"recommendation":"Legal review must happen before feature specification. This could constrain the solution space significantly — for example, GDPR data minimization may legally require approximate rather than exact location sharing."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Contradictory requirements from two authoritative stakeholder teams make the spec unimplementable as written.","business_rule_broken":"No coherent business rule can be derived when one team mandates 'always show exact GPS' and another mandates 'never show location.' These cannot both be true."},{"violation":"Legal/compliance stakeholder has not been consulted on worker location data sharing.","business_rule_broken":"Courier privacy and labor regulation compliance is a hard constraint that has not been evaluated. Any feature spec without this input is incomplete and potentially illegal."}]}
